name: VictoriaMetrics API
what_it_does: High-performance, open-source time-series database and monitoring solution with Prometheus-compatible API — providing long-term metrics storage, query (MetricsQL), and ingestion at lower cost and higher performance than Prometheus, with cluster and single-node variants.
use_cases: Storing long-term Prometheus metrics without the retention limitations
Querying time-series metrics via MetricsQL (Prometheus-compatible) API
Replacing Thanos or Cortex for scalable Prometheus long-term storage
Ingesting metrics from multiple Prometheus instances via remote_write
Building custom monitoring dashboards using VictoriaMetrics as data source
not_for: Log management (use Loki or Elasticsearch for logs)
Event tracing (use Jaeger or Tempo for distributed traces)
Complex event processing beyond time-series metrics
best_when: A team running Prometheus wants better performance and lower storage costs for long-term metrics retention without migrating from PromQL-based tooling.
avoid_when: You need logs or traces (not just metrics), full enterprise support without self-hosting, or have very simple metrics needs handled by Prometheus alone.

auth.notes: No auth on open-source self-hosted by default. VictoriaMetrics Cloud adds bearer token. Enterprise adds LDAP/Basic auth. Self-hosted — rely on network-level security (reverse proxy). API key on cloud version.

pricing.notes: Open-source (Apache 2.0) — self-hosted is fully free. Cloud SaaS for managed deployment. Enterprise for dedicated support and extra features. Ukrainian company (Kyiv). Very efficient storage (typically 10-15x better compression than Prometheus). Used by Wix, Grammarly, Roblox.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted — no rate limits; cloud limits per plan

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Prometheus-compatible REST API (instant query, range query, write, label queries). MetricsQL extends PromQL with additional functions. Good documentation. Self-hosted means no standard API endpoint. Both single-node and cluster variants. No auth by default — critical security consideration.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All metric reads fully idempotent. Metric ingestion is idempotent (same timestamp/label set updates existing). Query operations always idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: No default authentication — must add reverse proxy auth for production security
Self-hosted deployment — API endpoint varies per environment
MetricsQL is PromQL superset — mostly compatible but check for differences
Single-node vs cluster have different API endpoints and capabilities

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS configurable. No default auth — major security gap for production. Relies on network-level security or reverse proxy. Open-source Apache 2.0 — transparent codebase. Self-hosted means data stays in your infrastructure. Ukrainian company — check for geopolitical risk considerations.
